name: CrateDB Distributed SQL API
what_it_does: Distributed SQL database with REST API built for IoT, time-series, and analytics — combining document store with SQL query interface, horizontal scaling, and PostgreSQL wire protocol compatibility, optimized for high-ingestion workloads.
use_cases: High-throughput IoT sensor data ingestion and querying via SQL or REST HTTP
Time-series analytics at scale with SQL aggregations across millions of rows
Machine data and log analysis with full-text search and structured query support
Industrial data platforms requiring horizontal scale without schema migration pain
Real-time analytics on streaming data with distributed query execution
not_for: OLTP workloads requiring strong consistency and complex transactions (use PostgreSQL)
Simple key-value caching (use Redis or Valkey)
Graph database use cases requiring relationship traversal
best_when: An agent needs to query IoT, machine, or log data at massive scale with SQL, combining time-series performance with full-text search in a single distributed database.
avoid_when: You need strong ACID transactions, complex joins across normalized relational data, or a simple relational database.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth or PostgreSQL wire protocol auth. REST API uses Basic auth header. Superuser and normal user roles for access control. TLS configurable. Self-hosted auth managed per deployment.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: completely free; CrateDB Cloud: free trial
pricing.paid_tiers: CrateDB Cloud from ~$0.12/hour per node; Enterprise licenses available
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open source — free to self-host. CrateDB Cloud offers managed hosting. Strong IoT and industrial focus. EU-based (Austria). Enterprise support contracts available.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — self-hosted; Cloud tier based

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP REST API returns JSON for SQL queries. PostgreSQL wire protocol means psycopg2 and JDBC work. Standard SQL error codes. Good documentation with examples. Bulk insert via REST API efficient for agent data ingestion.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SELECT queries are idempotent. INSERT operations may create duplicates — use ON CONFLICT for upsert semantics. Bulk insert via COPY is not idempotent by default.
known_agent_gotchas: Eventual consistency for some operations — data may not be immediately visible after write
Not fully ACID compliant — distributed nature means relaxed consistency guarantees
REST API returns all results in memory — use LIMIT for large result sets
JVM-based — memory requirements higher than lightweight databases; plan node sizing

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully auditable. Basic auth — secure behind TLS. TLS configurable but not enforced by default. GDPR compliant. Self-hosted gives full data control. Austrian company with EU privacy standards.
